name: cable judo flip
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable handle with both hands at chest level, palms facing down.
Engage your core and rotate your torso to the right, pulling the cable across your body.
As you rotate, pivot your back foot and allow your hips to rotate naturally.
Extend your arms fully and finish the movement by flipping the cable handle over your shoulder.
Return to the starting position by reversing the movement, rotating your torso back to the center.
Repeat the movement on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the cable handle with both hands at chest level, palms facing down. Engage your core and rotate your torso to the right, pulling the cable across your body. As you rotate, pivot your back foot and allow your hips to rotate naturally. Extend your arms fully and finish the movement by flipping the cable handle over your shoulder. Return to the starting position by reversing the movement, rotating your torso back to the center. Repeat the movement on the opposite side. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
